regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, expect west winds at 11 knots accompanied by thunderstorms and a 10% rain probability, which may slightly affect embarkation and shore excursions but should not disrupt port operations. Port Everglades will see similar conditions with moderate west winds and minimal rain impact. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds shift to south-southwest at 10 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability in both Miami and Port Canaveral, providing excellent conditions for sailing and shore activities. By the third day, east-southeast winds increase to 13 knots with overcast skies and a 26% rain probability, which could potentially affect Tampa Bay shore excursions. Passengers and crew should monitor conditions beyond 48 hours as this slight increase in rain chance and cloud cover may influence operations along the western Florida coast.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, expect southwest winds at 18 knots accompanied by thunderstorms and a 36% rain probability that may affect port operations and shore excursions in Nassau, Freeport, and Bimini. Current conditions show lighter winds from the south-southwest at 5 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing for smooth embarkation and disembarkation this morning. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, south winds around 10 knots with a 30% chance of rain showers could impact activities in Key West, Grand Cayman, and Ocho Rios. By day three, easterly winds increasing to 15 knots along with a 53% rain probability indicate that rain showers are likely to affect port operations in Falmouth, Puerto Plata, and La Romana. Cruise vessels should prepare for fluctuating conditions, adjusting schedules as needed to accommodate potential weather disruptions.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, winds are southeast at 5 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, allowing for smooth port operations and minimal impact on shore excursions in San Juan and surrounding ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect east-southeast to east winds increasing to 9-12 knots with a drizzle and rain probabilities ranging from 15% to 26%, which may affect shore excursions in St. Thomas, St. Croix, and Antigua. By day three, easterly winds remain steady at 12 knots with drizzle and a 38% rain probability, which could affect operations in St. Maarten and St. Kitts. Passengers should prepare for slightly reduced visibility and damp conditions during upcoming port calls but no significant disruptions are forecast. Overall, wind speeds remain moderate, supporting steady vessel navigation throughout the Eastern Caribbean.
regions.western-caribbean: Today, an elevated rain chance of 98% with overcast skies is expected to impact Cozumel and Costa Maya, likely affecting port operations and shore excursions due to persistent wet conditions and light SSE winds at 6 knots. Current conditions show a south wind at 2 knots with overcast skies and a 30% rain probability, indicating increasing moisture as the day progresses. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, Roatan and Belize City could see a 41% rain probability with partly cloudy skies and ESE winds at 8 knots, which may cause intermittent interruptions to outdoor activities. Looking beyond 48 hours, Cozumel will experience ESE winds increasing to 10 knots accompanied by drizzle and a 35% rain probability, suggesting lingering damp conditions but reduced rainfall impacts for cruise operations. Passengers and crew should plan accordingly for wet weather today and monitor updates for gradual improvement starting tomorrow.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated east winds at 22 knots will affect Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability supporting stable port operations and shore excursions. In Cartagena, similar winds may impact vessel maneuvering but rain is not expected. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, these same ports will experience sustained east winds near 22 knots accompanied by drizzle, though rain probability remains below 10%, allowing most embarkation and disembarkation activities to continue with caution. Looking beyond 48 hours, Aruba and surrounding ports should prepare for continued east winds around 21 knots with a 47% chance of drizzle, which may affect shore excursions and outdoor activities. Cartagena’s conditions remain less impacted by rain but will see persistent elevated winds through this period.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today in Barbados, expect east winds near 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will strengthen to 14 knots accompanied by drizzle and a 50% rain probability, which may affect port operations in Barbados, St. Lucia, and Martinique as similar trade wind patterns extend across the region. On day three, drizzle will continue with east winds steady at 14 knots and a 47% rain probability, possibly impacting Antigua and St. Kitts as well. Passengers and crew should prepare for reduced visibility and damp conditions during these periods, with caution advised for outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, this trend of light drizzle and moderate east winds is expected to persist, warranting continued monitoring of port conditions throughout the Lesser Antilles.
regions.los-angeles: Today, winds are light from the west-southwest at 5 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing excellent conditions for embarkation and shore excursions at Los Angeles, San Pedro, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island. Into the next 24 to 48 hours, southwest winds will increase to 10 knots with overcast skies on Day 1, which may reduce sunshine but will not impact port operations at any local harbor. By Day 2, south winds around 7 knots will bring foggy conditions, primarily affecting visibility at Los Angeles and Long Beach, so vessels and excursions should anticipate limited sightlines during that period. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift to south-southeast at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies and continued less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth sailing and outdoor activities across the region through Day 3. Overall, no rain or strong winds are expected to disrupt cruise operations in the coming days.
regions.ensenada: Today in Ensenada, calm conditions prevail with east winds at 0 knots, clear skies, and less than 10% rain probability, allowing smooth embarkation and disembarkation as well as clear visibility for shore excursions at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift slightly to the southwest at 8 knots, with continued overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, presenting no significant changes to sea state or port activity for vessels and guests. Cruise ship maneuvering and shore excursions should proceed without weather-related concerns throughout this period.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, winds are light from the north-northeast at 2 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing excellent conditions for embarkation and shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz. By the third day, winds shift slightly to the west-northwest at 13 knots with continued overcast conditions and still less than 10% rain probability, so cruise vessel maneuvering and passenger activities near all three ports will remain largely unaffected. regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, winds are light from the east-southeast at 1 knot with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, providing excellent conditions for smooth port operations and shore excursions at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect winds to shift to the southwest at 7 knots and then to the south-southwest at 9 knots, with persistent overcast skies but still less than 10% rain probability, which will maintain safe and stable conditions for embarkation and disembarkation activities across all three ports. Beyond 48 hours, winds will continue from the south at 8 knots with overcast skies remaining and rain chances staying below 10%, indicating consistent conditions favorable for cruising and port logistics. Crew and passengers should anticipate calm seas and minimal weather disruptions throughout this period.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, thunderstorms with winds from the west-southwest at 12 knots are expected to impact Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, though the rain probability remains less than 10 percent. These storms could cause brief disruptions to shore excursions and port embarkation activities despite overall clear skies currently and light northeast winds at 2 knots. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, steady west-southwest winds at 12 knots and clear skies will prevail across all ports, supporting smooth cruise ship operations and shore visits. Beyond 48 hours, winds will shift to the west-northwest at 10 knots with partly cloudy skies, maintaining favorable conditions for ongoing cruising and port access in the region. Passengers and crew should remain aware of localized thunderstorm activity today but expect improving weather throughout the forecast period.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today in Barcelona and nearby Western Mediterranean ports like Valencia and Palma de Mallorca, winds are light from the west-northwest at 4 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, a significant weather event is expected with sustained north winds at 12 knots and thunderstorms bringing an 83% rain probability, which is expected to impact port operations and shore excursions especially in Barcelona, Valencia, and Palma de Mallorca. Mariners and passengers should prepare for increased wind and storm activity that could delay or alter scheduled port visits. By day three, winds will shift to the south at 8 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, indicating improving conditions but continued cloud cover. Cruise operations should monitor evolving conditions closely, particularly for the thunderstorms forecast on day two.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, thunderstorms with a 63% rain probability are expected to impact Naples and nearby ports such as Civitavecchia and La Spezia, with winds from the WSW at 11 knots and overcast skies likely to affect embarkation and shore excursions. Current conditions show a SW wind at 7 knots with an 11% rain probability and overcast skies, allowing for limited disruption this morning. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, the thunderstorm threat will increase with an 83% rain probability and SSW winds at 9 knots, affecting southern ports including Naples and possibly extending to Livorno and Sardinia, requiring operational adjustments. Beyond 48 hours, thunderstorms will intensify further with a 96% rain probability and WSW winds strengthening to 15 knots, suggesting widespread impacts on port operations and shore activities throughout the Central Mediterranean, including Monaco and Genoa. Passengers and crew should prepare for increasing rainfall and gusty winds impacting visibility and vessel maneuvering across the region.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds out of the west-northwest at 9 knots and clear skies with less than 10% rain probability will support smooth port operations and shore excursions in Athens and nearby Piraeus. Expect partly cloudy conditions tomorrow with a west wind at 9 knots and similarly low rain chances, which will maintain favorable conditions for calls at Santorini and Mykonos. By the third day, winds will shift to the south at 10 knots with overcast skies and a 16% rain probability; this could slightly affect visibility and excursion timing in Rhodes and Cyprus but remains manageable. Istanbul, Izmir, Haifa, and Alexandria will see minimal weather impacts during this period given these stable wind speeds and limited rain chances. Overall, cruise operations in the Eastern Mediterranean should proceed with routine scheduling and only minor adjustments by day three.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today, Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway are experiencing steady rain with a 100% probability, accompanied by southeast winds at 5 knots, which is expected to impact all shore excursions and port operations. The drizzle and light east-southeast winds at 4 knots currently reduce visibility, requiring extra caution during embarkation and tendering. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, rain with a 92% probability and light east winds at 5 knots will continue to affect Juneau and nearby ports, while drizzle conditions will also extend to Ketchikan and Skagway, potentially causing slower vessel maneuvering and dampened outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, a weakening rain chance of 53% with west-northwest winds at 5 knots suggests improving conditions, though drizzle may still affect some shore excursions in the region. Seattle’s embarkation weather today is not highlighted by rain, allowing for smoother boarding operations compared to the Alaska ports.
